title: Monitor API overview
text: Docusign Monitor helps organizations protect their agreements with round-the-clock activity tracking. The Monitor API delivers this activity tracking information directly to existing security stacks or data visualization tools—enabling teams to detect unauthorized activity, investigate incidents, and quickly respond to verified threats. It also provides the flexibility security teams need to customize dashboards and alerts to meet specific business needs.

title: Go-Live
text:
Before you can begin the Go-Live process, you must have a paid production Docusign account with a plan that includes Docusign Monitor.
When you are ready to start Go-Live review for your application, follow the steps described on the [Go-Live](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/esign-rest-api/go-live/) overview page for the Docusign eSignature REST API.

**Note:** If your application fails Go-Live review, you may be required to bring it into compliance with the [Rules and resource limits](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/esign-rest-api/esign101/rules-and-limits/) before you can Go-Live.

After the form is processed (which takes up to three business days), your integration key will be copied into production, enabling your app to call the production API endpoints.

title: Go-Live
text:
The developer and production endpoints for most Docusign APIs use slightly different paths. This table lists the endpoint base paths for each Docusign environment so you know how to modify your code when you migrate from the developer environment to production.

| Environment | API Base URI | Web Site Login URL |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Developer | `https://lens-d.docusign.net/api/v2.0/datasets/monitor/` | https://account-d.docusign.com |
| Production | `https://lens.docusign.net/api/v2.0/datasets/monitor/` | https://account.docusign.com |

 **Note:** To access production API endpoints, you will need to enable your integration key in the production environment. See [Go-Live](https://developers.docusign.com/platform/go-live/) for more information.

title: How-to guides overview
text:
The how-to guides in this section demonstrate how common [Docusign Monitor](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/monitor-api/monitor101/) scenarios work and how you can implement them into your own apps.  **Note:** Although the authentication process to obtain an access token is the same for both eSignature and Monitor APIs, you must request your token using JWT Authentication Grant and the caller must be impersonating either an **Organization Administrator** or a **Security Reports Administrator.** See [Docusign Monitor Authentication](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/monitor-api/monitor101/auth/) for details.

All API how-to guides assume you are developing with a Docusign developer account. A developer account is not specific to the [eSignature REST API](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/esign-rest-api/). If you have a developer account that you are using with our eSignature REST API, it will also work with other [Docusign APIs](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/). If you need to create a free developer account, select **Developer Account** from the menu at the top-right of the page, then **Create Account** or use the button below.

[Create account](https://www.docusign.com/developers/sandbox)

Each Monitor API how-to guide is listed below:

| **How-to guide** | **Code example source** |
| --- | --- |
| [How to enable Docusign Monitor for your organization](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/monitor-api/how-to/enable-monitor/) | N/A |
| [How to get monitoring data](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/monitor-api/how-to/get-monitoring-data/) | [Bash](https://github.com/docusign/code-examples-bash/blob/master/examples/Monitor/eg001GetMonitoringData.sh), [C#](https://github.com/docusign/code-examples-csharp/blob/master/launcher-csharp/Monitor/Examples/GetMonitoringDataFunc.cs), [Java](https://github.com/docusign/code-examples-java/blob/master/src/main/java/com/docusign/controller/monitor/services/GetMonitoringDataService.java), [Node.js](https://github.com/docusign/code-examples-node/blob/master/lib/monitor/examples/getMonitoringData.js), [PHP](https://github.com/docusign/code-examples-php/blob/master/src/Services/Examples/Monitor/GetMonitoringDataService.php), [PowerShell](https://github.com/docusign/code-examples-powershell/blob/master/examples/Monitor/eg001GetMonitoringData.ps1), [Python](https://github.com/docusign/code-examples-python/blob/master/app/monitor/examples/eg001_get_monitoring_data.py), [Ruby](https://github.com/docusign/code-examples-ruby/blob/master/app/services/monitor_api/eg001_get_monitoring_dataset_service.rb) |

title: Code example launchers
text: *Code example launchers* are complete projects that show common API integration functions. They enable you to see how to make API and SDK calls, how the code fits into an application, and how that code runs. You can freely clone, download, and run the launchers from the [Docusign GitHub](https://github.com/docusign). They are available in all of our supported SDK programming languages: C#, Java, Node.js, PHP, Python, and Ruby. Each launcher accesses Docusign APIs through the objects and methods available in its own language’s SDK. If you’d like to access the raw API (not with an SDK), we also have launchers available in Bash and PowerShell.

title: Code example launchers
text:
Most Docusign code examples contained in the code example launchers are also available with [Quickstart](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/esign-rest-api/quickstart/). Quickstart enables you to download a ZIP file containing the same set of Developer Center code examples in a specified language that you would get with a code example launcher, but it comes preconfigured with your account data and supports [Authorization Code Grant](https://developers.docusign.com/platform/auth/authcode/) and [JSON Web Token (JWT) Grant](https://developers.docusign.com/platform/auth/jwt/) authentication. This enables you to load and run the code examples without any additional configuration steps.

Both Quickstart and code launchers provide a web UI that runs locally and enables you to explore and run your desired code example by choosing the appropriate link.

Use code example launchers or Quickstart to:

- Develop your own integration, using the example as a model
- Learn best practices for implementing a scenario
- See how [Docusign Monitor Authentication](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/monitor-api/monitor101/auth/) works within a sample app

title: Code example launchers
text:
Each launcher has its own installation instructions, dependencies, and prerequisites. The README file for each launcher contains the specific details:

title: Code example launchers
text:
When executing Monitor API code examples, it’s useful to have test data in your developer account so that the responses contain realistic values. You can use the [MyAPICalls](https://myapicalls.sampleapps.docusign.com/) sample app to generate this test data. MyAPICalls has a collection of *scenarios,* or use cases, each of which includes a series of API requests. Executing a scenario once will generate one or more [tracked events](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gso1581445176035&topicId=crj1659119600990.html) that you can then retrieve using Monitor API requests. You can also configure MyAPICalls to run a scenario repeatedly using unique data each time, enabling you to quickly load a large volume of data into your developer account. This is helpful for testing and demonstrating dashboards that you’ve built with Monitor API calls. MyAPICalls sends API requests to the developer (demo) environment, not production.

Below is a list of MyAPICalls scenarios, and the tracked events that each one generates. To run any of the scenarios listed below, you can either access the scenario page directly via the link in the **Scenario** column, or you can visit the [MyAPICalls](https://myapicalls.sampleapps.docusign.com/) home page and select **Try scenario** for any scenario in the list. See the **Running the sample** panel on the scenario page for execution instructions.
> **Note:** The envelope signed event will be generated only if you complete the signing process for the envelope that the scenario creates.

| --- | --- |
| [Remote Signing with Template](https://myapicalls.sampleapps.docusign.com/scenario/1) | Template created Envelope created Envelope sent Envelope signed |
| [Embedded Signing](https://myapicalls.sampleapps.docusign.com/scenario/2) | Envelope created Envelope sent Envelope signed |
| [Responsive Signing](https://myapicalls.sampleapps.docusign.com/scenario/4) | Envelope created Envelope sent Envelope signed |
| [Phone Authentication](https://myapicalls.sampleapps.docusign.com/scenario/5) | Envelope created Envelope sent Envelope signed |
| [Create Connect Configuration and Receive Envelope Event](https://myapicalls.sampleapps.docusign.com/scenario/6) | Connect configuration created Envelope created Envelope sent Connect event sent Envelope signed |
| [Add a User](https://myapicalls.sampleapps.docusign.com/scenario/7) | Organization member added |
| [Composite Templates: Custom Forms with Shared Signature Document](https://myapicalls.sampleapps.docusign.com/scenario/9) | Template created Envelope created Envelope sent Envelope signed |

title: How to enable Docusign Monitor for your organization
heading_path: How to enable Docusign Monitor for your organization
text:
If you already have a developer account, move on to step 2.

To create a developer account:

1. At the top right of the Developer Center site, select **Create account**.
2. Enter your information, then use your provided email address to activate your new account.

title: How to enable Docusign Monitor for your organization
heading_path: How to enable Docusign Monitor for your organization
text:
To create an organization and gain access to the Monitor admin tile:

1. From the Docusign [developer environment](https://apps-d.docusign.com), select **Admin** at the top of the navigation bar.
2. In the admin tool, select **Get Started** on the **Docusign Admin is enabled for your company** banner.
3. If you do not already have an organization, choose the accounts to be linked to your new organization, then create the organization.
4. All demo accounts have access to the paid version of Monitor, and prod accounts might be entitled to the free version of Monitor. In Organization Admin, navigate to Features tab and toggle Docusign Monitor status to “Enabled.” If you’re having trouble accessing Monitor, reach out to your support manager or create a case in [Docusign Support Center](https://support.docusign.com/). Please note that the API for SIEM integrations is only available with the paid version of Monitor for prod accounts. Please contact sales if you’re interested in that.

title: How to get monitoring data
text:
This topic demonstrates how to get and display all of your organization’s monitoring data.

Run this code example from our [Quickstart](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/esign-rest-api/quickstart/) or launcher projects: [Bash](https://github.com/docusign/code-examples-bash), [C#](https://github.com/docusign/code-examples-csharp), [Java](https://github.com/docusign/code-examples-java), [Node.js](https://github.com/docusign/code-examples-node), [PHP](https://github.com/docusign/code-examples-php), [PowerShell](https://github.com/docusign/code-examples-powershell), [Python](https://github.com/docusign/code-examples-python), or [Ruby](https://github.com/docusign/code-examples-ruby).

title: How to get monitoring data
text:
Running the code in this how-to requires this data:

| Prerequisite | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Admin access | To call the Docusign Monitor endpoint, you must impersonate a user with administrator access to your organization.  This means that you must have obtained your access token using [JWT Grant](https://developers.docusign.com/platform/auth/jwt-get-token/) and have requested the impersonation [scope](https://developers.docusign.com/platform/auth/reference/scopes/).  **Note:** The impersonated administrator may not be an admin of more than one organization. Admins of two or more organizations who call the Monitor API endpoint will receive responses with the 403 Forbidden [error code](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/esign-rest-api/esign101/error-codes/). |
| Your organization must have access to Docusign Monitor | Your organization must have access to Docusign Monitor to call the endpoint.  The Monitor API is available for developer (demo) accounts for testing and development, but for apps in the production environment, it is a separate SKU that requires Enterprise Pro. For more information about how to enable the Monitor API for your account, contact Docusign Support. |

title: How to get monitoring data
text:
To make the API calls shown in this how-to, you need a valid OAuth access token.

Because Docusign Monitor requires that you impersonate a user with admin access to your organization, you must have obtained your access token using [JWT Grant](https://developers.docusign.com/platform/auth/jwt-get-token/) and have requested the impersonation [scope](https://developers.docusign.com/platform/auth/reference/scopes/). For details, see [Docusign Monitor Authentication](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/monitor-api/monitor101/auth/).

title: How to get monitoring data
text:
Make a GET request to the [DataSet:getStream](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/monitor-api/reference/monitor/dataset/getstream/) endpoint to get your monitoring data. Because your organization may have large amounts of data, you can use the `limit` query parameter to specify the maximum number of records each call will return (2,000 in the example shown here) and a `cursor` value to continue the query from where the previous one left off.

In this example, we return and display chunks of monitoring data, record the returned cursor value, and use that value to begin the next query until all data has been returned.

title: Monitor API 101
text:
Docusign Monitor helps organizations protect their agreements with round-the-clock eSignature and CLM activity tracking. The Monitor API delivers this activity information in JSON chunks directly to your existing security stack, integrating easily with the software you’re already using, including:

- Security Information and Event Management (SIEM) tools like Splunk or Sumo Logic
- Data visualization tools like Tableau or Power BI

Integrating Monitor with these tools gives your team flexibility to tailor dashboards and alerts to meet your specific industry, security, and regulatory requirements.

You can see a video walkthrough for how to integrate Docusign Monitor with Splunk below:

title: Monitor API 101
heading_path: Requirements to use the Monitor API
text:
Monitor tracks eSignature and CLM account activity at the organization level, and therefore requires you to define an organization within Docusign Admin. Subject to these prerequisites, some Docusign Monitor functionality is available for developer (demo) accounts to test in the Monitor Free Edition version, while the paid edition of Docusign Monitor (that includes the Docusign Monitor API) is available as a separate product.

The free version of Docusign Monitor does not support the Monitor API, [SIEM](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Security_information_and_event_management) integration (including the [Docusign Monitor Splunk App](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?rsc_301=&bundleId=gso1581445176035&topicId=sfj1611351664499.html)), or CLM events and alerts.

Contact [Docusign Support](https://support.docusign.com/s/contactSupport) for more information about how to enable the Monitor API.

title: Authentication
text:
Before your application can make calls to the Monitor API, it must authenticate and obtain an access token. You must submit this access token, which proves your app’s identity and authorization, with each request.

To get an access token with the appropriate credentials to make calls to the Monitor API, you must use [JWT Grant](https://developers.docusign.com/platform/auth/jwt-get-token/). This is because:

- The JWT Grant is the only OAuth2 Grant that enables you to impersonate a consenting user when making API calls, and
- To obtain tracking data for your organization, the caller must be impersonating either an **Organization Administrator** or a **Security Reports Administrator**, and must not be administrator in any other organization. For information about permission profiles, see **Docusign Admin permission profiles** in [Organization Administrators](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?language=en_US&rsc_301&bundleId=rrf1583359212854&topicId=god1583359152535.html&_LANG=enus).

The process to authenticate to use the Monitor API is identical to the process for calling the eSignature REST API using the JWT Grant, with the following exceptions:

- When you obtain consent to impersonate a user to make calls on their behalf, you must impersonate a user with the **Organization Administrator** or a **Security Reports Administrator** role for your organization. The User ID of this user will be included in the sub property of the body of the JWT.
- The scopes that you request in the body of the JWT must include `signature` and `impersonation`. See [Authentication scopes](https://developers.docusign.com/platform/auth/reference/scopes/) for the full list of scopes.

See [How to get an access token with JWT Grant](https://developers.docusign.com/platform/auth/jwt-get-token/) for a walkthrough of getting an access token using JWT Grant.

title: Best practices for handling alerts
text:
Docusign Monitor includes pre-built alerts for common types of suspicious activity, and enables you to create custom alerts using the Monitor API. Because alerts can be triggered by malicious or authorized activity, we provide several options so you can appropriately respond to potential threats as they are investigated and resolved.

Sample alert types:

- User fails six or more login attempts in an hour
- Admin disables a user-authentication requirement
- Admin initiates a bulk user export
- User downloads 20 or more envelopes in an hour
- User logs in from a location more than 100 km from any previous location

title: Best practices for handling alerts
text: When an administrator or security operations team receives an alert from Monitor, they should act quickly to investigate the alert and respond appropriately. For example, if a user fails too many consecutive login attempts, an administrator could reset that user’s password—or take one of the following actions:

title: Best practices for handling alerts
text:
If an account has been flagged as compromised, you can close that account to prevent additional activity while you investigate.

**Required privileges:** Docusign Administrator, or eSignature Administrator on the same organization account as the user.

To close the account:

1. Select **Users** in the main menu.
2. Select the user from the display.
3. From the **Actions** menu, select **Close**.

For more information, see [Manage eSignature users](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?language=en_US&rsc_301&bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=ixb1583277365937.html&_LANG=enus).

title: Best practices for handling alerts
text:
If a delegated admin account is flagged as compromised because of suspicious activity, you can revoke admin privileges while you investigate.

**Required privileges:** Docusign Administrator

To revoke the user's admin privileges:

1. From the **Docusign Admin** dashboard, click **Administrators**.
2. Locate the administrator you want to remove.
3. From the **Actions** menu, select **Remove**.
4. Choose **Confirm**.

Alternatively, you can change the user profile as described in the User Details section in [User Management](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?language=en_US&rsc_301&bundleId=rrf1583359212854&topicId=jni1583359185567.html&_LANG=enus).

title: Best practices for handling alerts
text:
If an account shows signs of suspicious activity, you can notify the account owner and force a password reset to help prevent unauthorized access.

**Required privileges:** eSignature Administrator on the user's account

To force a password reset:

1. Select **Users** in the main menu.
2. Select the user from the display.
3. From the **Actions** menu, select **Reset Password**.

For more information, see the Reset User Password section in [Manage Users](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?language=en_US&rsc_301&bundleId=rrf1583359212854&topicId=jni1583359185567.html&_LANG=enus).

title: Best practices for handling alerts
heading_path: Transfer a user's envelopes and templates
text:
If a user account has been compromised, you can transfer their envelopes and templates to a secure user account.

**Required privileges:** eSignature Administrator on the user's account

To transfer a user's envelopes and templates:

1. Select **Users** in the main menu.
2. Select the user from the display.
3. From the **Actions** menu, select **Transfer Envelopes** or **Transfer Templates**.

For more information, see the Transfer envelopes or templates between users section in [Transfer Envelopes or Templates](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?language=en_US&rsc_301&bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=vyz1583277376194.html&_LANG=enus).

title: Best practices for handling alerts
heading_path: Revoke the user's eSignature administrator privileges
text:
If an eSignature admin account is flagged as compromised because of suspicious activity, you can revoke eSignature admin privileges while you investigate.

**Required privileges:** Docusign Administrator

To revoke a user's eSignature administrator privileges:

- From the **Docusign Admin dashboard**:
  1. Select **Users** and search for the user by email address. The user’s profile appears.
  2. In the **Actions** tab, select **Edit**.
  3. In the **Enable Applications** section, in eSignature select a new permission profile for the user.
- From the **Monitor Alert History** page:
  1. When the **Alert History** panel is opened for a particular alert, choose **Manage User**. The user’s profile appears.
  2. In the **Actions** tab, select **Edit**.
  3. In the **Enable Applications** section, in eSignature select a new permission profile for the user.

For more information see the View and manage details for a user section in [User Management](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?language=en_US&rsc_301&bundleId=rrf1583359212854&topicId=jni1583359185567.html&_LANG=enus).

title: Best practices for handling alerts
text:
If envelopes were transferred from a potentially compromised account, and that account has been cleared after an investigation, those envelopes can be restored to the original user.

**Required privileges:** eSignature account administrator

To restore a user's envelopes:

1. Select **Docusign eSignature Agreements** tab in the main menu.
2. In the **Envelopes** section, select **Deleted**.
3. Select the deleted envelopes and then move them to the desired folder.

title: Events and alerts
text:
Docusign Monitor tracks eSignature and CLM account activity across your organization in near real-time. This activity is made up of *events* resulting from the actions taken by users, administrators, and APIs connected to your organization.

When specific sets of events occur in your organization (typically events that indicate possible suspicious activity), Docusign Monitor can trigger an *alert*. When an alert is triggered, the activity that caused the alert is recorded in Monitor or your SIEM system, and the appropriate administrator or security operations team member is automatically notified.

Monitor includes alerts for common types of suspicious activity—such as a user deleting a large number of envelopes in a short period of time, or removing a signer authentication requirement. Security teams can also leverage the Monitor API and their existing security stack to create custom alerts that meet their organization and regulatory requirements.

See [Monitor event properties](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gso1581445176035&topicId=lcd1585600758257.html) for details on the properties of a monitor event.

title: Tracking data and handling alerts
text:
You can get activity tracking information for your organization by calling the Docusign Monitor API [DataSet:getStream](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/monitor-api/reference/monitor/dataset/getstream/) endpoint. This endpoint enables you to download your organization’s data in segments specified by a cursor (a bookmark value returned by a previous `DataSet:getStream` operation). You can use the resulting dataset to integrate the Monitor API with your existing SIEM software tools and apps.

- In the development (demo) environment, the endpoint is:
  `https://lens-d.docusign.net/api/v2.0/datasets/monitor/stream?cursor={{cursorValue}}&limit={{queryLimit}}`

- In the production environment, the endpoint is:
  `https://lens.docusign.net/api/v2.0/datasets/monitor/stream?cursor={{cursorValue}}&limit={{queryLimit}}`

title: Tracking data and handling alerts
text:
When you call the [DataSet:getStream](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/monitor-api/reference/monitor/dataset/getstream/) endpoint, you can supply two parameters, `cursor` and `limit`, that determine the subset of the monitoring data that will be returned in the response JSON.

**Note:** To call the Monitor API endpoint, you must have obtained an access token using JWT authentication. See [Authentication](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/monitor-api/monitor101/auth/) for details.

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `cursor` | A `string` query parameter that specifies the place in your monitoring data from which the request will begin gathering records. Your app can use `cursor` to keep its place while iterating through monitoring records in a way similar to how you might use a bookmark to keep your place while reading chapters of a book. Whenever you successfully call the Monitor API endpoint, the response will include an `endCursor` value which you can use to create the `cursor` for your next request, enabling you to chunk your downloads as you examine large numbers of event and alert records.  Calls that do not specify a `cursor` will begin retrieving records starting seven days ago.  If data is needed beginning at a certain point in time, `cursor` can be specified as an ISO DateTime string value (such as 2022-01-01T00:00:00Z) instead of a cursor query string. In this case, data will be retrieved beginning from that DateTime. The earliest data available in the system is from 2021-01-01.  Docusign does not return all available data in response to a single request. Your integration should iterate through the dataset using a series of API calls, with each one requesting data starting from the `cursor` value and the subsequent request starting from the `endCursor` in the response.  **Note:** If a request returns no data, your integration should continue iterating using `cursor` and `endCursor` until data is returned. To avoid throttling, do not make requests more frequently than once per minute.  Not required. Defaults to 0. |
| `limit` | An `Int32` query parameter that specifies the maximum number of records (up to 2000) to be returned in the response. Not required. Defaults to 1000.  This parameter enforces a maximum number of records returned but does not guarantee a minimum number of records. |

After a successful call, a set of JSON event and alert records is returned, including an `endCursor` metadata property.

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `endCursor` | A `string` value that specifies the place at which the monitoring data in the current response ended.   Use the value of `endCursor` to begin your next query from this spot in your records. |

[DataSet](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/monitor-api/reference/monitor/dataset/)

title: : getStream
text:
Gets customer event data for the organization that owns the integration key.

The results for this endpoint are paginated by their event ingestion time, which can be up to five minutes after the event originally occurred. Use the `cursor` parameter to specify where the query begins in the dataset. Use the `limit` parameter to set the number of records returned.

For a detailed example of how to use `getStream` and retrieve monitoring data, see our guides [How to get monitoring data](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/monitor-api/how-to/get-monitoring-data/) and [Tracking data and handling alerts](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/monitor-api/monitor101/tracking-data-handling-alerts/).

**Note**: data is not guaranteed to be in the order of the timestamp of the events. It is returned in the order it was inserted into our database.

title: : getStream
text:
- [How to get monitoring data](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/monitor-api/how-to/get-monitoring-data/)
- [Tracking data and handling alerts](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/monitor-api/monitor101/tracking-data-handling-alerts/)

title: : getStream
text:
GET

```
/api/v{version}/datasets/{dataSetName}/stream
```

title: : getStream
text:
| Path Parameters |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| dataSetName \* | string | Must be `monitor`. |
| version \* | string | Must be `2`. |

| Query Parameters |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| cursor | string | Specifies a pointer into the dataset where your query will begin. You can either provide an ISO DateTime or a string cursor (from the `endCursor` value in the response to the previous request). If no value is provided, the query begins from seven days ago.  For example, to fetch event data beginning from January 1, 2022, set this value to `2022-01-01T00:00:00Z`. The response will include data about events **ingested** starting from that date in chronological order. The response also includes an `endCursor` property. To fetch the next page of event data, call this endpoint again with `cursor` set to the previous `endCursor` value.  **Note:** If a request returns no data, your integration should continue iterating through the dataset. See [Tracking data and handling alerts](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/monitor-api/monitor101/tracking-data-handling-alerts/) for more information. |
| limit | integer | The maximum number of records to return. The default value is 1000. The maximum permitted value is 2000.  **Note:** this is the maximum, but you may get fewer items based on your dataset and cursor position. |

\* Required

title: Protect your agreements with activity tracking
heading_path: Protect your agreements with activity tracking
Get near real-time alerts with Docusign Monitor
text:
Preventing damage from unauthorized activity is a major challenge for organizations. While Docusign meets or exceeds stringent U.S., EU and global security standards, your agreements can only be as secure as your organization’s credential management and operational integrity.

Monitor provides near real-time visibility into these factors as they relate to your Docusign agreements and processes. Using advanced analytics to track [Docusign eSignature](https://www.docusign.com/products/electronic-signature) web, mobile, and API account activity across the organization in near real-time, Docusign Monitor empowers teams to strengthen security operations.

- Detect potential threats

title: View Alert Details with Monitor Alert History
heading_path: View Alert Details with Monitor Alert History
text:
View and customize detailed Monitor alert information using the Alert History filters and
options.

Use this feature to view the Alert History and detailed information for each alert.
Additionally, you can take investigative action based on the alert activity.

For example, if a user has multiple login failures, and you suspect suspicious activity,
you can: contact the user or lock that user's access to prevent further actions.

1. From your Docusign home page, select Admin > Organization > Monitor from the side menu.
2. Select Alert History to display alerts.

   Tip: You can refine the alert
   display details by:
   - Selecting the date range
   - Specifying one or more alerts

   ![A screenshot of a sample Monitor Alert History page.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/gso1581445176035/page/images/mon-new-alert-hx.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTc1MzgsInNoZWFmIjoiZ3NvMTU4MTQ0NTE3NjAzNSJ9.I8_cNbvu0FqTteLCjYe_Sr9nv5efCM3XaXL20Nr43Pc&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of a sample Monitor Alert History page.")
3. Select an
   alert's
   overflow (three-dot) menu to view actions you can take.

   ![A screenshot of the Alert History overflow menu options.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/gso1581445176035/page/images/mon-al-hx-menu.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTc1MzgsInNoZWFmIjoiZ3NvMTU4MTQ0NTE3NjAzNSJ9.I8_cNbvu0FqTteLCjYe_Sr9nv5efCM3XaXL20Nr43Pc&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Alert History overflow menu options.")
4. Optionally select additional actions from the overflow (three-dot) menu on each
   alert row in the Alert Historydisplay. You can quickly investigate and manage alerts
   without leaving Monitor.

   Perform one or more of these actions:

title: View Alert Details with Monitor Alert History
heading_path: View Alert Details with Monitor Alert History
Download Alert History to a CSV File
text:
To take a snapshot of Monitor alert information, use the Download Alert History
feature to store the details in a CSV file.

Note:

- When you select this option, Monitor creates a `.zip`  that
  includes a CSV for the specified alerts and a CSV for events.
- Downloading a CSV file is not available in Monitor Free Edition.

1. Select Alert History to display alerts.
2. Select the criteria for the information download.

   - Time frame
   - One, multiple, or all alerts
3. Select Download Alert History.

   This example shows a sample Alert History CSV.

   ![A screenshot of a sample alert history CSV.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/gso1581445176035/page/images/mon-new-csv.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTc1MzgsInNoZWFmIjoiZ3NvMTU4MTQ0NTE3NjAzNSJ9.I8_cNbvu0FqTteLCjYe_Sr9nv5efCM3XaXL20Nr43Pc&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of a sample alert history CSV.")

title: Use Docusign Monitor Free Edition
text:
Learn about how to access and start using Docusign Monitor Free Edition.

Docusign Monitor Free Edition is available for all Docusign organizations in Docusign Organization Admin.
This only applies to organizations that aren't already paying for Monitor.

ISV and Resellers are excluded.

1. Access the Docusign Admin dashboard for your account.
2. Select Monitor.
3. Complete the Monitor Free Edition Onboarding Wizard.
4. Select Get Started to access the Monitor dashboard where you
   can:
   - Use the User Investigations tab to review alert activity
     over the last seven days.
   - Use the Alert History tab to view historical alert
     information
   - Use the IP Safe List  tab to create a list of trusted IP
     addresses

With Monitor Free Edition, you work with limited Monitor features. At any time, you can buy
Monitor to take advantage of all functionality by contacting [Docusign sales](https://www.docusign.com/contact-sales) to upgrade.

title: View Monitor Free Edition Alert History
heading_path: View Monitor Free Edition Alert History
text:
Learn how to view the Docusign Monitor Free Edition alert activity for up to 7 days from
the dashboard.

Use Alert History to view the alert activity data.

1. Select Admin > Organization > Monitor from the side menu.
2. Select Alert History.
3. Select an activity date range for the Alert History display.

   The data date range display choices are 24 hours or
   7 days.
4. View alert details and recommended actions as described in the [View Alert Details with Monitor Alert History](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gso1581445176035&topicId=OLDorv1594064852688.html&_LANG=enus "View and customize detailed Monitor alert information using the Alert History filters and options.") article.

title: Use the Docusign Monitor API
text:
Learn about using the Monitor API to send data directly to the application or website
you choose to integrate with Docusign.

Integrate the Docusign Monitor API to receive a real-time stream of event data for
your Docusign account.

The Docusign Monitor API operates on the same principles as the Docusign’s API stack,
so trust that your data remains secure, reliable and up to date.

For detailed information about the Monitor API, see the [Monitor
API Overview](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/monitor-api/) page.

title: Manage Alert Notifications
text:
Learn how to add additional recipients or prevent specific Docusign
user from receiving alert notifications. Use this feature to reduce email traffic from users
who should not receive alert notifications.

Within each alert's settings, you can specify which organization or security report
admins receive Monitor alerts. If you do not specify any email recipients, all
organization administrators and Security Reports administrators receive alert
notifications by default.

You can also prevent specific users from triggering alert notifications by adding them to a safelist. For example, you can
prevent shared accounts from triggering the Simultaneous login alert.

See the [Monitor alerts](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gso1581445176035&topicId=refd45c3b91-27da-49ee-aba6-f946c7e203da.html&_LANG=enus "Review descriptions and recommended actions for Docusign Monitor eSignature, Admin settings, and login and security alerts.") article for a list of alerts.

These customized settings are only available to organizations with the full version of
Monitor.

1. From the Docusign home page, select Admin > Organization > Monitor from the side menu.
2. Select Settings to display the alerts.
3. Select Edit next to the alert that you want to
   configure.
4. In the  Edit Alert dialog box, update the fields as
   described in the [Configure Alert Threshold
   Parameters](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gso1581445176035&topicId=bzc1620336954182.html&_LANG=enus "Adjust the alert sensitivity or event thresholds that generate alerts for your deployment.") article.

   ![A screenshot of a sample Edit alert details dialog box.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/gso1581445176035/page/images/mon-new-man-al-settings..png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTc0MTcsInNoZWFmIjoiZ3NvMTU4MTQ0NTE3NjAzNSJ9.FKYxQWhQ_uwxKe8QHyurSl1Oyv8OwocjVRP6Gbr2gFI&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of a sample Edit alert details dialog box.")
5. Optionally select users to customize the alert notification settings:
   - Alert only these recipients to specify who gets
     the alert notifications.
   - Prevent users from triggering alert to prevent
     alert notifications when the users take action that may generate the
     alert.
6. Select Save.

title: Use Docusign Add-on Workflow Actions to Search
heading_path: Use Docusign Add-on Workflow Actions to Search
text:
Use the Docusign Add-on app's workflow actions to create investigation dashboards to
track Monitor events through the Splunk dashboard.

The Docusign Add-on app provides workflow actions that open investigation dashboards directly
from Splunk searches. The workflow actions are:

- docusign\_alert\_workflow
- docusign\_envelope\_workflow
- docusign\_user\_workflow

The following example shows the workflow actions.

![Image of Docusign Add-on workflow actions](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/gso1581445176035/page/images/monitorsplunkworkflow.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTc1MjcsInNoZWFmIjoiZ3NvMTU4MTQ0NTE3NjAzNSJ9.OyUs2E1ExObD7uY-8lVyuIRu6fa5LQnCXP86H--GnX4&_LANG=enus)

title: Use Docusign Add-on Workflow Actions to Search
heading_path: Use Docusign Add-on Workflow Actions to Search
text:
Once you expand an event in Splunk, you can use the drop down to use the workflow
action. These workflow actions are only visible for specific event types and in
certain fields as shown in the following example.

![Screen shot of Docusign Add-on workflow action data.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/gso1581445176035/page/images/monitorsplunkworkflowdata.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTc1MjcsInNoZWFmIjoiZ3NvMTU4MTQ0NTE3NjAzNSJ9.OyUs2E1ExObD7uY-8lVyuIRu6fa5LQnCXP86H--GnX4&_LANG=enus)

Here are the different workflow actions and the event types and fields with which they are
associated.

| This workflow action: | Applies to this event type: | Applies to this field: |
| --- | --- | --- |
| docusign\_alert\_workflow | docusign\_alert\_event\_type | alert user |
| docusign\_envelope\_workflow | docusign\_envelope\_event\_type | data.envelopeid user |
| docusign\_user\_workflow | docusign\_monitor\_event\_type | user |

title: Use Docusign Add-on Workflow Actions to Search
heading_path: Use Docusign Add-on Workflow Actions to Search
text:
The values in the docusign\_cim\_lookup correlate actions and objects with events, and
is based on the Docusign Monitor Schema documentation

There are some fields that are only present in certain events. For example, results of
successes and failures only apply to user logon data.

The following example shows the Docusign User Investigator dashboard. Notice that
although there are Events Over Time there are no
corresponding Event Results Over Time. This occurs because
the types of events listed (Envelope Created / Sent) have no associated result
value. When this occurs, the display includes the note:

`Only certain events have an associated <fieldname>`

![Screen shot of Docusign User Investigator dashboard example.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/gso1581445176035/page/images/monitorsplunkdsuserdash.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTc1MjcsInNoZWFmIjoiZ3NvMTU4MTQ0NTE3NjAzNSJ9.OyUs2E1ExObD7uY-8lVyuIRu6fa5LQnCXP86H--GnX4&_LANG=enus)

This type of message also displays for:

- Top Affected User in the Docusign Account Investigator dashboard
- Events Results Over Time in the Docusign User Overview dashboard

title: Create Account and User List Lookup Tables
heading_path: Create Account and User List Lookup Tables
text:
Configure the Monitor account and user lookup tables to track these activities in
Splunk.

Create and configure accounts and user list lookup tables.

1. From the App, select Settings > Searches, reports and alerts >  Docusign – Generate Account Lookup.

   Running this search generates
   docusign\_account\_list.csv.
2. Enable the Docusign - Generate Account List Lookup
   search so it is automatically refreshed daily.
3. Run the Docusign – Generate Users Lookup search to generate
   the docusign\_users\_list.csv.
4. Enable the Docusign – Generate Users List Lookup search
   so it is automatically refreshed daily.

   Note the following:

   - The searches are dependent on data collected for the docusign\_account
     and docusign\_users sourcetypes. A sourcetype is a field that identifies
     the data structure of an event. The indexer identifies and adds the
     source type field when it indexes the data. If this data has never been
     indexed, Splunk does not create a lookup table.
   - The searches run once overnight by default. You can set a frequency that is
     specific to your organization.

title: Splunk Troubleshooting for Docusign Monitor Setup
heading_path: Splunk Troubleshooting for Docusign Monitor Setup
text:
Learn about technical resolutions for common setup and data ingestion errors that you may
encounter when configuring the Docusign Monitor Splunk Add-on
(TA).

Here are technical resolutions for common setup and data ingestion errors that you may encounter
when configuring the Docusign Monitor Splunk Add-on (TA).

title: Splunk Troubleshooting for Docusign Monitor Setup
heading_path: Splunk Troubleshooting for Docusign Monitor Setup
text:
Docusign Monitor supports the latest Splunk platforms.

Prior versions (9.2.x and earlier) with older add-ons may exhibit dependency and
library errors. Upgrade Splunk and the Docusign Monitor Add-on to
avoid recurring failures.

The Latest Add-on is available in [Splunkbase](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/).

**Resolution**

- Deploy the latest Docusign Monitor Add-on (TA)
  version to mitigate known Python JWT and cryptography dependency loading
  failures.
- Where feasible, upgrade the Splunk platform to **9.3+**.

Known errors include:

- `No module named '_cffi_backend'`
- `'str' object has no attribute 'decode'`
- Python JWT/cryptography dependency loading issues. Monitor license
  and organization entitlement

title: Splunk Troubleshooting for Docusign Monitor Setup
heading_path: Splunk Troubleshooting for Docusign Monitor Setup
text:
**Organization entitlement validation**

Confirm that Docusign Monitor is globally enabled at the
**Organization level**. Monitor is an organization-wide product, not a
single-account feature.

In Account Admin, verify that the client app has the required scopes and
reissue credentials if they are updated.

- `signature`
- `impersonation`
- `organization_read`

If the Admin dashboard shows Monitor, but Splunk widgets fail with a 403
error, it is usually a scope and entitlement mismatch: not a network outage.

**Propagation Delay**

Allow adequate time (typically a few hours) for entitlement caches to
propagate across Demo and Production environments after the initial activation.

title: Splunk Troubleshooting for Docusign Monitor Setup
heading_path: Splunk Troubleshooting for Docusign Monitor Setup
Required OAuth scopes for the client application
text:
**Mandatory Scopes**

The client application that Splunk uses must possess, at a minimum, these
OAuth scopes:

- `signature`
- `impersonation`
- `organization_read`

**403 Errors**

A 403 Forbidden status on Monitor data inputs is the most frequent symptom
of insufficient scopes or entitlement, even if token authentication succeeds.

title: Splunk Troubleshooting for Docusign Monitor Setup
heading_path: Splunk Troubleshooting for Docusign Monitor Setup
text:
**Input endpoints problem**

The Splunk Add-on requires three distinct data inputs with unique external
endpoints:

- `Account Users`
- `Organization Accounts`
- `Org Events`

**Diagnosis**

If ingestion fails for a single input while others succeed, the root cause
is typically an endpoint misconfiguration. It could also be a missing scope for that
specific resource.

title: Splunk Troubleshooting for Docusign Monitor Setup
heading_path: Splunk Troubleshooting for Docusign Monitor Setup
RSA key format and parsing issues
text:
**Key format error**

If this error is returned.

`Could not parse the provided public key`

Verify that the key is encoded as PKCS#8 in the standard Privacy-Enhanced
Mail (PEM) delimiter:

`-----BEGIN PUBLIC KEY----- … -----`

`END PUBLIC KEY-----`

**Resolution**

Regenerate the key using the correct format and re-upload by selecting:

Client Applications > Manage Secrets > Upload Public Key.

1. Regenerate keys in the correct format:

   1. Use PKCS#8 public key (`BEGIN PUBLIC KEY`),
      not SSH-format.
   2. Upload through Client Applications > Manage Secrets.
2. Upload the public key again and re-test. Parsing errors generally
   indicate format issues, not Monitor issues.

title: Splunk Troubleshooting for Docusign Monitor Setup
heading_path: Splunk Troubleshooting for Docusign Monitor Setup
Wrong resource URLs or local host calls
text:
**Symptoms**

- 404 Not Found
- Splunk attempts call to 127.0.0.1:8089 for Docusign
  resources

**Solution**

1. Update Splunk Data Inputs to the correct external endpoints per the
   Add-on.
2. Remove any `localhost/default`  REST manager
   path.
3. Ensure that proper `docusign_accounts`,
   `docusign_users`, and monitor events inputs are
   configured.

title: Splunk Troubleshooting for Docusign Monitor Setup
heading_path: Splunk Troubleshooting for Docusign Monitor Setup
text:
**Co-location**

The Splunk App and the Docusign Monitor Add-on must run
on the **same Splunk instance and region**. Mismatched deployment can result in
empty dashboards, even if event ingestion logs confirm data receipt.

**Index Validation**

Confirm the configured target index and sourcetypes (such as
`sourcetype=docusign:monitor)` align with both the data input
settings and dashboard configurations.

title: Splunk Troubleshooting for Docusign Monitor Setup
heading_path: Splunk Troubleshooting for Docusign Monitor Setup
text:
**Initial data ingestion window Retention Policy**

The newly configured Splunk App initiates data retrieval only for the
**last seven days of events**, followed by continuous forward accumulation.

The Monitor Splunk app does not offer Historical backfill. It is not an
automated feature.

**Troubleshooting**

If dashboards display zero values:

- Verify that qualifying events exist within the last seven days.
- Generate a new event to confirm successful ingestion beyond the setup
  phase.

**Validation**

To validate that Docusign Monitor events are polled
through the Splunk Add-on, run this internal audit query:

```
|  |  |
| --- | --- |
|  | index=_internal sourcetype=docusign* sourcetype="docusignmonitor" log_level=* |
|  | "events_received=" |
```

**Diagnostic Scenarios**

Zero Events through Add-on (In Docusign Admin)
:   If events\_received=0 despite the Docusign Admin UI
    reporting activity within the last 7 days:

    - **Data Retention/Sync:** Verify that the polling
      interval is within the seven-day ingestion window.
    - **Trigger test:** Manually generate a new audit
      event in the Docusign Admin UI to force a
      state change.

Architectural Alignment
:   Ensure the Splunk App and Add-on are **co-located** on the same search
    head/instance and mapped to the same region.

Dashboard Nulls (Successful Ingest)
:   If raw data exists but dashboards are vacant, validate **search
    macros** and **lookup tables** (such as
    `docusign_accounts` or
    `docusign_users`). Missing lookups break the schema
    mapping for high-level visualizations.

title: Splunk Troubleshooting for Docusign Monitor Setup
heading_path: Splunk Troubleshooting for Docusign Monitor Setup
text:
**Issue**

Discrepancy between user visibility in the Docusign
Admin and the Splunk User Lookup dashboard.

**Root Cause**

The User Lookup dashboard is dependent on **account-specific input streams**.
Splunk does not automatically discover all accounts under an organization. It only
indexes users for specific Account IDs configured within the Add-on.

**Resolution**

1. **Account IDs:** Specify all relevant Account IDs within the Docusign Organization.
2. **Input Configuration:** Explicitly configure each Account ID as
   an individual input in the Splunk Add-on.
3. **Lookup Refresh:** Re-run the lookup generation
   scripts/searches to populate the user metadata tables for the newly added
   accounts.

title: Splunk Troubleshooting for Docusign Monitor Setup
heading_path: Splunk Troubleshooting for Docusign Monitor Setup
Useful query to check Splunk logs
text:
Confirm that Monitor ingest activity (Add-on internal logs) with this
query:

```
|  |  |
| --- | --- |
|  | index=_internal sourcetype=docusign:modinput:* |
|  | sourcetype="docusign:modinput:monitor" log_level=* events_received" |
```

The query verifies polling and the count of events received on each
call.

title: Splunk Troubleshooting for Docusign Monitor Setup
heading_path: Splunk Troubleshooting for Docusign Monitor Setup
text:
If you completed the troubleshooting steps and ingest still fails after you
corrected entitlement, scopes, keys, versions, and endpoints:

- Collect logs and screenshots and file **a customer support ticket
  at** [**support.docusign.com**](http://support.docusign.com/)**.**
- **Include:**

  - Add-on and App versions
  - Configured indexes and sourcetypes
  - Key provisioning method
  - Recent events timeline (to confirm 7-day window
    behavior).
  - Screenshots and full context
  - Scope listing
  - Splunk platform version

title: Use the Monitor Free IP Safe List
heading_path: Use the Monitor Free IP Safe List
text:
Learn about how to use the Configure IP
Safe List feature to create a list of trusted IP addresses in Docusign Monitor Free Edition.

configure any IP Address ranges, the alert does not generate.

title: Monitor Use Cases
text:
Read about examples where Docusign Monitor tracks and alerts you
about suspicious activity.

Monitor tracks and notifies you about these types of use cases.

- An administrator removes a security policy from any user (such as the policy to enforce SSO
  login)

  Potential threat: Unauthorized access and data loss

  Action:
  Contact Docusign the administrator to verify the activity was intentional and
  warranted
- A user logs in from a location from which they haven’t logged on from in the last 90
  days

  Potential threat: Malicious outside hacker

  Action: Notify user, force logoff, change password
- A user tries to access or delete more than 50 envelopes in a day

  Potential threat:
  Unauthorized access or data loss

  Action: Contact the administrator to
  verify the activity was intentional and warranted
- Docusign REST API activity increases suddenly

  Potential threat: Compromised credentials
  leading to an unauthorized flow of data

  Action: Contact the administrator
  to verify the activity was intentional and warranted

title: Customize the Alert Date Range

7. Select the Calendar Icon.
8. Select the date range that you want as shown in this example.

   ![A screenshot of Monitor Alert Details Custom Date Picker.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/gso1581445176035/page/images/monnewcustdaterange.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTc0MjksInNoZWFmIjoiZ3NvMTU4MTQ0NTE3NjAzNSJ9.35UcaoK4299LnnUJ9TsojsZERiswEWx5Sm-qFwEkSTU&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of Monitor Alert Details Custom Date Picker.")

title: Access the Monitor Free Edition Dashboard
heading_path: Access the Monitor Free Edition Dashboard
text:
Learn how to access, view, and interpret the Docusign Monitor Free Edition
Dashboard.

Use the Monitor dashboard to view data about your organization's events. You
can:

- View a 30-day summary of Core metrics, including envelope use and user activities
- View events and event details
- Enable or disable alerts
- View alert details and historical information

Additionally, you can create filters to view the time frame for the alert data:

- 24 hours
- 7 days

Note: For Monitor activity tracking:

- Monitor only tracks user activity if the users have an email address that is
  in a domain that your organization has claimed.
- Docusign Admin does not allow adding users to accounts if they are not members of a claimed
  domain for an account's with the Prevent Unmanaged
  Signups setting enabled. Also, Docusign Monitor does not
  track these user activities. For detailed information, see Prevent
  unmanaged signups in [Administrator Checklist Before Enforcing SSO﻿](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=rrf1583359212854&topicId=azf1636145324908.html&_LANG=enus).

1. From your Docusign home page, select Admin > Organization > Monitor from the side menu.

   Note: If you are using Monitor for the first time, the
   Monitor Free Edition wizard displays. The wizard provides an overview of
   Monitor Free Edition features. Select Next to move
   through the feature descriptions.
2. Use the dashboard to view your account's activity and access other Monitor
   features.

title: Receive Docusign Monitor Alert Email Notifications
heading_path: Receive Docusign Monitor Alert Email Notifications
text:
Learn about how Monitor sends email alerts for security events.

By default, Docusign organization administrators receive email alerts when
the alert events occur, enabling organization administrators to quickly review and
respond to potential threats.

Note: User events require the Monitor-enabled organization to claim the email
domain that is associated with that user as described in the [Add and Verify a Subdomain to Use
as a Custom Email Domain](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=rrf1583359212854&topicId=kpy1624481491369.html&_LANG=enus) article. This ensures that the organization is authorized to view that
user's information. If you reserve no email domains, you do not receive information
about these events.

Unless you select specific users as described in the [Select Alert Email Recipients](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gso1581445176035&topicId=loo1631913386767.html&_LANG=enus "For each alert, specify which users receive Docusign Monitor alert email notifications.") article, these Docusign organization
administrators receive alert email notifications:

- Docusign organization administrator.
- Delegated administrator with the Security Reporting Delegated Administrator role. See the
  [Add a Docusign
  Administrator](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=rrf1583359212854&topicId=cvy1583359150599.html&_LANG=enus) article for information about setting up this role.

Additionally, you can take investigative action based on the alert activity.."), where you can view details and take action.

title: Docusign Monitor Versions Comparison
text:
Compare the Docusign Monitor Free Edition introductory features to Monitor's extensive
features.

Docusign Monitor Free Edition provides a low-risk opportunity to experience the value and
functionality of Monitor. You can use Monitor Free Edition to determine how you might
use Monitor in your organization.

Important:
Docusign Monitor Free
Edition is a pilot program that is currently ongoing for existing customers. It is not
available for new Docusign Monitor customers.

Compare the features of the Free Edition of Docusign Monitor to Monitor.

Table 1.
Docusign Monitor Free Edition and Monitor Comparison

| Feature | Monitor Free Edition | Monitor |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Alerts | 3 | 24 |
| Events | 12 | 106 |
| Data display time frame | 30 days maximum | Since November 2020 |
| Dashboard widgets | - Login activity: Only world map display - Administrator and user settings: Only line chart display | Select from these display modes:  - Bar chart - Line chart - Table - World map |
| Advanced features | No access | - [Create custom   widgets](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gso1581445176035&topicId=psb1620335565782.html&_LANG=enus "Configure Docusign Monitor deployment alert sensitivity or event thresholds to customize when and how Monitor generates your organization alerts.") - [Download alert   history](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gso1581445176035&topicId=OLDorv1594064852688.html&_LANG=enus "View and customize detailed Monitor alert information using the Alert History filters and options.") - [Download event   history](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gso1581445176035&topicId=fap1613668711861.html&_LANG=enus "Take a snapshot of Monitor events by using the Download CSV feature to store Monitor event details in a CSV file.") |
| API | No access | [Monitor API](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/monitor-api/) |
| SIEM integration | None | [Splunk](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gso1581445176035&topicId=sfj1611351664499.html&_LANG=enus "Use the Docusign Splunk App and Docusign Add-on to directly deliver Docusign Monitor notifications to Splunk.") |

Note: Both Monitor versions require a claimed domain for your organization.
For more information, see the [Claim a Domain](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=rrf1583359212854&topicId=gso1583359141256.html&_LANG=enus)
article.

title: Configure Monitor Data Inputs
text:
Configure Monitor account, monitor, and user data inputs to display in your Splunk
dashboard.

With Splunk data inputs, you can insert data sources (rest API) into Splunk. Use the Docusign Add-on menu to configure these Docusign
Monitor data inputs:

There must be a separate data input for each account in the organization.

Note: To estimate data size, each streamed record is approximately
500 Bytes in size. The total number of events is approximately 10 events for every
envelope sent. However, volume varies based on product activity.

- Docusign Accounts
- Docusign Monitor
- Docusign Users

1. Select Settings >  Data Inputs.

   ![A screenshot of Configure Monitor data inputs types, number, and actions.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/gso1581445176035/page/images/monitor_splunk_config_dataInputs.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTc0OTcsInNoZWFmIjoiZ3NvMTU4MTQ0NTE3NjAzNSJ9.leC072f8UJ6sN4RQF4Yel8zOvKMDMy27gi-dcyt_jZk&_LANG=enus)

   Note: You can add the data into
   any index but make sure that all data is collected to the same index.
2. Select +Add new for Docusign Accounts.
3. Enter this information:

   | Information | Provided by: |
   | --- | --- |
   | Name | User |
   | User ID | Docusign |
   | Integrator key | User generated - See your API and key configuration. |
   | RSA private key | User generated - See your API and key configuration. |
   | Auth Server | Docusign Monitor system |
   | Lens Server URL | Docusign Monitor system |
   | fetchLimit | User |

   This example shows a completed Accounts data input entry:

   ![A screenshot of Sample data input field entries for Accounts data.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/gso1581445176035/page/images/monsplunkdatainput.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTc0OTcsInNoZWFmIjoiZ3NvMTU4MTQ0NTE3NjAzNSJ9.leC072f8UJ6sN4RQF4Yel8zOvKMDMy27gi-dcyt_jZk&_LANG=enus)

   Note:
   - For every account in your organization, add a new account and enter the
     account information as described in the previous steps.
   - For integrating with your developer (Demo) Docusign
     account to test the integration, you must enter the demo environment
     information for Auth Server and API Server
     URL.
   - For your production environment, you must promote your Demo account
     integration key to a production Docusign account as
     described in the [Go-Live](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/esign-rest-api/go-live/) topic. Once your integration key is
     promoted, use production URLs (removing the -d  in
     the field entries).

   Here is the configuration information for Demo and Production environments for Docusign Accounts, Monitor, and Users source types.

   - Demo environment URLs
     - Auth Server: `https://account-d.docusign.com/oauth/token`
     - API Server/Get Organization Accounts:
       `https://api-d.docusign.net/management/v2/organizations`.
     - Get Account Users:
       `https://demo.docusign.net/restapi/v2.1/accounts/{{Account_Id}}/users`.

       Replace
       the {Account\_Id}  with your account ID
       information.
     - Lens Server:
       `https://lens.docusign.net/api/v2.0/datasets/monitor/stream`
   - Production environment URLs
     - Auth Server: `https://account.docusign.com`
     - API Server/Get Organization Accounts:
       `https://api.docusign.net/management/v2/organizations`.
     - Get Account Users:
       `https://{server}.docusign.net/restapi/v2.1/accounts/{Account_Id}/users`

title: Monitor Dashboard Features
text:
Read about the security monitoring features available in the Docusign Monitor dashboard. Using these features, you can customize the alerts for events you want to monitor and choose how they display the information.

With the Monitor dashboard, you can view and collect data about your organization's events. You can customize the type of event alerts to track and set up the alert data display. Specifically, you can:

- View events graphical overview and details with the Dashboard
- Review recent user activity with User Investigations
- View alert details and historical information with Alert History
- View and configure trusted IP addresses with the IP Safelist
- Enable or disable alerts with Settings

For example, use the Monitor dashboard to view these types of events:

- Mass envelope deletion: Checks for mass deletions of agreements
- Recipient authorization removal for viewing envelopes: This can indicate questionable activity or user error
- Multiple user logon failures: This can indicate user error or questionable activity

Additionally you can create a filter to view the alert interval for the alert data:

- 24 hours
- 7 days
- 30 days
- 60 days
- 90 days
- Year to date
- 365 days
- Custom time range

Note: For Monitor activity tracking:

- Monitor only tracks user activity if the users have an email address that is in a domain that your organization has claimed.
- If the account's Prevent Unmanaged Signups setting is enabled, Docusign Admin does not permit adding users to accounts if they are not members of a claimed domain. Additionally, Docusign Monitor does not track these user activities. For detailed information, see the [Administrator Checklist Before Enforcing SSO](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=rrf1583359212854&topicId=azf1636145324908.html&_LANG=enus) article.
- Monitor captures activities such as envelope sends or document downloads that are associated with the account regardless of user status.

This example shows the Monitor dashboard.

![A screenshot of a sample Docusign Monitor Dashboard.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/gso1581445176035/page/images/mon-uinvest-dash.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTczNjgsInNoZWFmIjoiZ3NvMTU4MTQ0NTE3NjAzNSJ9.KKNcJnARViIlOLmQVf6nmeyBjAZhpxiPAixHigD61no&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of a sample Docusign Monitor Dashboard.")
