title: Configure the Blocklist Lookup Tables
text:
Configure the blocklist lookup tables to track Monitor blocklist activity.

Use the block list lookup tables to set and block
locations or IP ranges that are considered suspicious. Docusign recommends using the
Splunkbase Lookup File Editor, available on the [Splunk App for Web
Analytics](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/2699/) page, to configure lookup tables.

Note the following:

- All locations' values are set to FALSE by default. This
  indicates they're not suspicious. Set suspicious locations' value to
  TRUE.
- The docusign\_ip\_lookup does not include any predefined
  ranges. Enter this information to use the Block List Overview dashboard.

1. From the App, select Setting > Lookups.
2. From the Lookups page, select Lookup table
   files.
3. Using the provided path, edit
   docusign\_location\_lookup.csv and set suspicious
   locations to TRUE.

   Note:

   The changes in this example can also be made with the Lookup Editor. Find and
   edit files in `\Splunk\etc\apps\docusign_ta\lookups`.

   ![A screenshot of sample blocklist lookup table.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/gso1581445176035/page/images/monitorsplunkcountrylookup.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTc1MTcsInNoZWFmIjoiZ3NvMTU4MTQ0NTE3NjAzNSJ9.E_O8H5LBv9soDiFL3R3y23zmZUz69n6hHSQFDtoRhAc&_LANG=enus)
4. Using the Lookup Editor, enter your organization’s subnets and label them as
   FALSE to easily identity traffic from inside the
   network.

   An automatic lookup uses information in the lookup table and assigns fields to all
   docusign:monitor data if there is match. Use these
   fields to identify suspicious events.

   | Blocklist lookup | Field | Field created |
   | --- | --- | --- |
   | docusign\_ip\_lookup | Ip | ip\_blocklisted |
   | docusign\_location\_lookup | location | location\_blocklisted |

title: Error Message: API Data Error and Lens Unauthorized Reference ID Errors
heading_path: Error Message: API Data Error and Lens Unauthorized Reference ID Errors
text:
Troubleshoot an error that occurred when attempting to get API data or a Lens reference
ID in the Docusign Monitor Splunk dashboard.

This error occurs when attempting to get API data or a Lens reference ID in the Docusign Monitor Splunk dashboard. Lens is the Monitor API endpoint (where the programs
communicate with each other).

You receive this error
message:

```
Error occurred when getting API data or error getting lens data unauthorized
reference id.
```

The user does not have the required permissions to get API data or a Lens
reference ID.

title: Error Message: API Data Error and Lens Unauthorized Reference ID Errors
heading_path: Error Message: API Data Error and Lens Unauthorized Reference ID Errors
API Data error and Lens data unauthorized reference ID error
text:
Responsible party: Docusign organization
administrator

In Docusign Administration, assign Administrator
or Security Reports Admin permissions to the
user.

title: Select Alert Email Recipients
text:
For each alert, specify which users receive Docusign Monitor alert email
notifications.

You can specify which users receive alert email notifications for each alert.

Note:

- For both Monitor Paid and Monitor Free editions, only oraganization administrators or security
  administrators can receive email notifications.

  If you do not specify any email
  recipients, organization administrators and Security Reports administrators
  will receive alert email notifications by default.
- If your organization has the Monitor Paid edition, you can restrict alerts to send only to specific email addresses entered here.

1. From your Docusign home page, select Admin > Organization > Monitor from the side menu.
2. Select Alert List.
3. Select the alert for which you want to customize email notifications.
4. Select Edit.
5. Select Alert Email Recipients.
6. Begin typing into display the first recipient name.

   ![A screenshot of selecting a Monitor alerts email recipient.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/gso1581445176035/page/images/monnewselemail.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTc1NDgsInNoZWFmIjoiZ3NvMTU4MTQ0NTE3NjAzNSJ9.bvzp7fdYxROThnBuzP4bVF06X8Kc5_z-OzcvUhzzfvM&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of selecting a Monitor alerts email recipient.")
7. Select additional recipients and select SAVE.

   ![Screen shot of selected Docusign Monitor alert notifications recipients.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/gso1581445176035/page/images/monnewemailsave.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTc1NDgsInNoZWFmIjoiZ3NvMTU4MTQ0NTE3NjAzNSJ9.bvzp7fdYxROThnBuzP4bVF06X8Kc5_z-OzcvUhzzfvM&_LANG=enus "Screen shot of selected Docusign Monitor alert notifications recipients.")

title: Change an Alert's Default Risk Status
heading_path: Change an Alert's Default Risk Status
text:
Learn about changing Docusign Monitor alerts' default risk status to refine the number
and frequency of the alerts you receive.

Based on your experience as an administrator, you can change the alert risk status
assigned to Monitor alerts in the Alert List tab.

The monitor includes these alert severity assignments:

- Auto: System default for when to take action
- High: Take action within a few hours
- Medium: Take action within one day
- Low: Action may or may not be required

To set alert severity assignments:

1. From your Docusign home page, select Admin > Organization > Monitor from the side menu.
2. Select Settings  to display alerts.
3. For the alert you want to change, select Edit.
4. In the Set Default Risk Level dialog box, select a default
   risk level.

   ![A screenshot of a sample Monitor Risk Threshold dialog box.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/gso1581445176035/page/images/mon-env-del-thresh-db.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTc0MzQsInNoZWFmIjoiZ3NvMTU4MTQ0NTE3NjAzNSJ9.OnqRo-FzoE2a1Qi3hfhGdGkf1EABLRQK75Yd2b8o7BE&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of a sample Monitor Risk Threshold dialog box.")
5. Enter a number for the Event Threshold. In this example,
   you're setting the maximum number of envelope deletions before generating the
   alert.
6. Optionally assign the alert's email notifications by entering the responsible
   administrator's email address.

title: Configure Approved Docusign IP Addresses
text:
Learn how to create a list of trusted IP addresses with the Docusign
Monitor IP Safe List feature.

As a security administrator, use the Configure IP Safe
List feature to create a list of trusted IP addresses.

Note the following:

- This feature only applies to the  IP address outside accepted ranges
  alert.
- Enable this alert in the Monitor Settings page as described in the [Set Up Notifications in Docusign Monitor Settings](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gso1581445176035&topicId=ovg1582324205227.html&_LANG=enus "View, enable, or disable Docusign Monitor security alerts for your deployment .") article.
- Add IP addresses in the Monitor Safe List to generate an IP address
  outside allowed ranges alert. If you do not configure any IP
  Address ranges, the alert does not generate.

Note: Users should access their accounts through controlled IP
addresses. Avoid adding individual IP addresses.

1. From your Docusign home page, select Admin > Organization > Monitor from the side menu.
2. Select IP Safe List to display the IP Address Safe List
   window.
3. Select Add to Safe List. Any configured acceptable IP
   addresses display.

   ![A screenshot of the Monitor IP Safe List dashboard with approved IP addresses.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/gso1581445176035/page/images/mon-new-ip-safe-list-pg.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTc0NDAsInNoZWFmIjoiZ3NvMTU4MTQ0NTE3NjAzNSJ9.dAhuxppxOiyf_yCQV2QUfV3xCzuukGrMW47NjTZanr8&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Monitor IP Safe List dashboard with approved IP addresses.")

   If there are no approved IP addresses, the IP Safe List display is empty,
   with the option to add IP addresses.

   ![A screenshot of the Add to Safe List message.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/gso1581445176035/page/images/monnewaddsafelistdb.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTc0NDAsInNoZWFmIjoiZ3NvMTU4MTQ0NTE3NjAzNSJ9.dAhuxppxOiyf_yCQV2QUfV3xCzuukGrMW47NjTZanr8&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Add to Safe List message.")
4. Select  Add to Safe List to create new acceptable IP
   addresses.
   1. In the Add IP address dialog box, enter an IP
      address.

      ![A screenshot of a dialog box to add approved Monitor IP addresses.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/gso1581445176035/page/images/monnewaddtosafe.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTc0NDAsInNoZWFmIjoiZ3NvMTU4MTQ0NTE3NjAzNSJ9.dAhuxppxOiyf_yCQV2QUfV3xCzuukGrMW47NjTZanr8&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of a dialog box to add approved Monitor IP addresses.")
   2. Optionally enter the last IP address in the range that you want to
      include.

      Tip: You can enter a Classless
      Inter-Domain Routing (CIDR) address in this field.

      CIDR notation
      is shorthand for the subnet mask. The CIDR address looks like a
      normal IP address, except that it ends with a slash followed by
      a number. The number after the slash represents the number of
      addresses in the range. For example, enter the CIDR address
      128.0.0.0/24.

      ![A screenshot showing entering approved Monitor IP addresses using a CIDR IP address range.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/gso1581445176035/page/images/monnewaddiprange.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTc0NDAsInNoZWFmIjoiZ3NvMTU4MTQ0NTE3NjAzNSJ9.dAhuxppxOiyf_yCQV2QUfV3xCzuukGrMW47NjTZanr8&_LANG=enus "A screenshot showing entering approved Monitor IP addresses using a CIDR IP address range.")

      Note: When entering the CIDR address, there may be
      a slight delay before the Last IP Address field completes the
      Last IP Address field.
   3. Select Save.

title: Configure Approved Docusign IP Addresses
text:
a CIDR IP address range.")

      Note: When entering the CIDR address, there may be
      a slight delay before the Last IP Address field completes the
      Last IP Address field.
   3. Select Save.

      The IP address or range of addresses displays in the IP Safe
      List.

      Note: Manually refresh the IP Safe List
      page to reflect your changes.
5. Edit an existing IP address or range of addresses.
   1. Select an IP address entry and select ACTIONS > Edit.

      ![A screenshot of a dialog box for Monitor edit IP address options with Edit or Remove options.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/gso1581445176035/page/images/monitoripeditoptions.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTc0NDAsInNoZWFmIjoiZ3NvMTU4MTQ0NTE3NjAzNSJ9.dAhuxppxOiyf_yCQV2QUfV3xCzuukGrMW47NjTZanr8&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of a dialog box for Monitor edit IP address options with Edit or Remove options.")
   2. Change the first, last, or CIDR address IP address fields.
   3. Select SAVE.
   4. Optionally delete the entry by selecting ACTIONS > Remove.

      ![A screenshot of the Remove from Monitor Safe List confirmation message with Remove or Cancel options.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/gso1581445176035/page/images/monitoripremove.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTc0NDAsInNoZWFmIjoiZ3NvMTU4MTQ0NTE3NjAzNSJ9.dAhuxppxOiyf_yCQV2QUfV3xCzuukGrMW47NjTZanr8&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Remove from Monitor Safe List confirmation message with Remove or Cancel options.")

      The system displays a confirmation message to remove the IP address or IP address
      range.
   5. Select Remove to delete the IP address from the
      IP Address Safe List.

      The command prompts you to confirm removing the IP addresses.

      `Are you sure you want to remove the IP range? This cannot be
      undone`

      Manually refresh the IP Safe List page to display to reflect your
      changes.

title: Configure Approved Docusign IP Addresses
text:
Search the Docusign Monitor IP Safe List to determine whether
an IP address has already been submitted.

When you add IP addresses to your safe
list, they display in the Monitor IP Safe List. All IP addresses are considered
unauthorized unless you add them to the Safe List.

1. From Docusign Admin, select Monitor.
2. Select IP Safe List. Any configured acceptable IP
   addresses display.
3. Select Search IP Addresses to locate existing IP
   Safe List addresses.

   ![A screenshot of the Monitor IP Safe List dashboard with existing approved IP addresses.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/gso1581445176035/page/images/monitoripsafedash.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTc0NDAsInNoZWFmIjoiZ3NvMTU4MTQ0NTE3NjAzNSJ9.dAhuxppxOiyf_yCQV2QUfV3xCzuukGrMW47NjTZanr8&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Monitor IP Safe List dashboard with existing approved IP addresses.")

If the IP address for which you searched does not display in the
IP Address Safe List, optionally add it.

title: Configure Approved Docusign IP Addresses
text:
As a security administrator, you want to view activity from unknown or unapproved
locations. The Docusign Monitor dashboard includes a tile with
event and envelope activity for IP addresses that are not specified in the
Monitor IP Safe List.

1. From Docusign Admin, select Monitor.
2. Select IP Safe List.

   The unauthorized IP Address activity displays under the IP Safe List dashboard.

   ![A screenshot of a Monitor dashboard with an unauthorized IP Address Activity display.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/gso1581445176035/page/images/monnewunauthip.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTc0NDAsInNoZWFmIjoiZ3NvMTU4MTQ0NTE3NjAzNSJ9.dAhuxppxOiyf_yCQV2QUfV3xCzuukGrMW47NjTZanr8&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of a Monitor dashboard with an unauthorized IP Address Activity display.")

   These types of activities over the last 90 days from any unauthorized IP addresses display
   in these widgets in the dashboard:

   - Unauthorized events
   - Unauthorized events by location
   - Unauthorized events by name
3. View the alert history and detailed information as described in the [View Alert Details with Monitor Alert History](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gso1581445176035&topicId=OLDorv1594064852688.html&_LANG=enus "View and customize detailed Monitor alert information using the Alert History filters and options.") article.

title: Monitor Free Edition Features
text:
Learn about the Docusign Monitor Free Edition features.

Monitor Free Edition demonstrates the powers of Monitor by letting you work with
limited
features.

You need Docusign Organization Admin Basic (The free version
of Organization Management) to use this product.

This article summarizes Free Edition Monitor features.

title: Monitor Free Edition Features
text:
The Free Edition Monitor dashboard provides:

- A 30-day summary of Monitor Free Edition core metrics, including the number of:
  - Events
  - Alerts
  - Active users
  - Login events
  - Envelopes deleted
- Up to a 7-day summary in:
  - Alerts: Notify you when specific sets of events occur in your
    organization
  - Events: Specific activities in your organization
  - Envelope deletion activity: User deletes twenty or more envelopes in one hour,
    indicating possible unauthorized access or data loss
  - Log on activity: Successful or failed logon attempts
  - User tracking:
    - Changes in user information, such as email address, password, profiles,
      or recovery information
    - Activity such as account access, or account lockout
- Configurable dashboard widgets that display and track alert activity

Note:

With Monitor Free Edition, you cannot:

- Assign other admins to receive email notifications
- Add additional email recipients
- Add ignored users assigned to the paid version of Monitor to the Monitor Free
  version

![A screenshot of the Docusign Monitor Free Edition dashboard.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/gso1581445176035/page/images/mon-newest-free-dash.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTczOTQsInNoZWFmIjoiZ3NvMTU4MTQ0NTE3NjAzNSJ9.H_v2uyysvtWXRVu5INMbJzA-gC54GH5hQNux8oHUhhQ&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Docusign Monitor Free Edition dashboard.")

title: Monitor Free Edition Features
text:
Monitor Free Edition provides these alerts:

- Password changed from new location
- Recipient authentication disabled
- Unusual envelope deletion volume

See the [Docusign Monitors](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gso1581445176035&topicId=refd45c3b91-27da-49ee-aba6-f946c7e203da.html&_LANG=enus "Review descriptions and recommended actions for Docusign Monitor eSignature, Admin settings, and login and security alerts.") article for detailed information about these
and other Monitor eSignature alerts.

title: Monitor Free Edition Features
text:
Monitor Free Edition provides these events:

See the [Docusign Monitor Events](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gso1581445176035&topicId=orf1734466223875.html&_LANG=enus "Learn about the security events that Docusign Monitor tracks.") for detailed information about these
and other Monitor eSignature events.

- Administrator added
- Delegated administrator added
- Envelope created
- Envelope deleted
- Envelope voided
- Login failure
- Login success
- Member added to organization
- Member invited to an account
- Member profile updated in Organization
- Password changed
- Password rule updated

title: Configure the Alerts Lookup Table
text:
Configure the Monitor blocklist lookup tables to track alerts.

Configure alert severity using the Alerts Lookup
table. Docusign recommends using the [Splunk App
for Lookup File Editing](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1724) to configure lookup tables.

1. Access the docusign\_alert\_lookup table using the Splunk
   lookup editor.
2. Set the severity and severity id using these values only.

   | severity | severity\_id |
   | --- | --- |
   | low | 1 |
   | medium | 2 |
   | high | 3 |
   | critical | 4 |

   Note: By default, all alerts are
   set to medium severity. Set the
   severity and severity\_id in the
   docusign\_alert\_lookup.csv based on your organization’s
   requirements.

title: Use Filters to Customize Monitor Displays
heading_path: Use Filters to Customize Monitor Displays
text:
Review how Docusign Monitor filters let you customize Monitor displays to view the data
you need.

Filters let you change and customize Monitor events and alert data displays.

You can filter an alert tile display on the Monitor dashboard.

![A screenshot of sample display filters.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/gso1581445176035/page/images/mon-newest-filter-page.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTc1NDMsInNoZWFmIjoiZ3NvMTU4MTQ0NTE3NjAzNSJ9.frLSWDgt8Dja9cYyCPmlrrhFkqp1ATYLYer_Gm_7fz0&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of sample display filters.")

title: Use Filters to Customize Monitor Displays
heading_path: Use Filters to Customize Monitor Displays
text:
You can filter the alert display by selecting from the drop-down list for:

- Alerts ID: Select one or more alerts to view the alert
  history. The default is All Alerts.
- Event ID: Select one or more events to view the alert
  history. The default is All Alerts.
- User ID: Select one or more events to view the alert
  history. The default is All Alerts.
- Alert History time range
  - Last 24 hours
  - Last 7 days (default)
  - Last 30 days
  - Last 60 days
  - Last 90 days
  - Last 6 months
  - Year to date
  - Last 365 days

title: Use Filters to Customize Monitor Displays
heading_path: Use Filters to Customize Monitor Displays
text:
You can customize the information on Monitor's configurable dashboard tiles that
display and track events.

![A screenshot of a sample Monitor display tile filter menu.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/gso1581445176035/page/images/mon-newest-tile-display-menu.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTc1NDMsInNoZWFmIjoiZ3NvMTU4MTQ0NTE3NjAzNSJ9.frLSWDgt8Dja9cYyCPmlrrhFkqp1ATYLYer_Gm_7fz0&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of a sample Monitor display tile filter menu.")

Select the three-dot menu to modify the display:

- Edit: Display and change the event filter information.
- Refresh Data: Updates the display information.
- Remove from Dashboard: Removes the tile from the Monitor dashboard display.

title: Use Filters to Customize Monitor Displays
heading_path: Use Filters to Customize Monitor Displays
text:
From the User Investigations > Event  > Event Details display, you can add or remove data from the display by selecting a
filter.

- Search users: Specify one or more users by username or
  email address.
- Use the Date range field to select the data time
  frame:
  - 7 days
  - 30 days
  - 90 days
  - Last 12 months
  - A custom date range
- Accounts field: Specify one or more accounts
- Customize Columns icon ![A screenshot of the Customize columns icon.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/gso1581445176035/page/images/mon-cust-col-icon.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTc1NDMsInNoZWFmIjoiZ3NvMTU4MTQ0NTE3NjAzNSJ9.frLSWDgt8Dja9cYyCPmlrrhFkqp1ATYLYer_Gm_7fz0&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Customize columns icon.")): Add or remove display columns.

  ![A screenshot of a sample Monitor Customize columns dialog box.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/gso1581445176035/page/images/mon-cust-cols.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTc1NDMsInNoZWFmIjoiZ3NvMTU4MTQ0NTE3NjAzNSJ9.frLSWDgt8Dja9cYyCPmlrrhFkqp1ATYLYer_Gm_7fz0&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of a sample Monitor Customize columns dialog box.")

Select Clear all to remove your customizations.

Widget display options: Use these options to change the
display format by selecting the filter and entering the information:

- Show: Filter by which events display in the widget (the
  default is All).

  See the [Monitor Alerts](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gso1581445176035&topicId=refd45c3b91-27da-49ee-aba6-f946c7e203da.html&_LANG=enus "Review descriptions and recommended actions for Docusign Monitor eSignature, Admin settings, and login and security alerts.") and the [Monitor
  Events](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gso1581445176035&topicId=crj1659119600990.html&_LANG=enus "Review the details for Docusign Monitor eSignature events.")

  articles for a list and descriptions of alerts and
  events you can include.
- IP Addresses: Include the following IP Address
  information. See the [Configure Approved Docusign IP Addresses](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gso1581445176035&topicId=mfq1645630427780.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to create a list of trusted IP addresses with the Docusign Monitor IP Safe List feature.") article for
  information about configuring the IP Safe List.
  - All (default)
  - All IP Addresses in Safe List
  - All IP Addresses outside Safe List
- Grouped by: The information is filtered by the following
  event information:

  Note: This option may be disabled
  depending on the selected chart type.

  - Overall (default)
  - User
  - Account
  - IP Address
  - Event Name
  - Browser
  - Operating System
  - Device Type
  - Integrator key
- Displayed as: Widget's display format
  - Line Chart
  - Bar Chart
  - World Map
  - Table

![A screenshot of event widget filter options.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/gso1581445176035/page/images/monfilterwidgetdisp.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTc1NDMsInNoZWFmIjoiZ3NvMTU4MTQ0NTE3NjAzNSJ9.frLSWDgt8Dja9cYyCPmlrrhFkqp1ATYLYer_Gm_7fz0&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of event widget filter options.")

You can also customize the displays as described in the [Customize the Monitor Dashboard Display Tables](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gso1581445176035&topicId=yjb1633967629712.html&_LANG=enus "Customize which parameter columns display in a table and the order where table columns display in Docusign Monitor dashboard table views.") topic, as well as changing the order in which
table columns appear in Docusign Monitor dashboard table
views.

title: Monitor Data Streaming
text:
Learn about calling the Monitor streaming endpoint to download data to your SIEM or
database.

Once you have successfully authenticated calls as described in the [Monitor API Authentication](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gso1581445176035&topicId=qxp1582325276688.html&_LANG=enus "Learn about how the Monitor API authenticates calls through OAuth 2.0 authentication and a JWT grant.") article, you can call the Monitor streaming endpoint to
begin downloading data into a SIEM or database of your choice.

See the [How to get monitoring data](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/monitor-api/how-to/get-monitoring-data/) page for information about
downloading data.

title: Docusign Monitor Events
text:
Learn about the security events that Docusign Monitor
tracks.

Docusign

Transmit the event data to your Security information and event management (SIEM) to establish event thresholds as described in the [Integrate a SIEM System article](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gso1581445176035&topicId=uxz1581533216990.html&_LANG=enus "Integrate your SIEM to receive security alerts directly from Monitor.").

- Dubious attempts to access accounts
- Unusual envelope activity patterns
- Authentication anomalies

Note:

- Monitor does not record login failure events for organizations using SSO.
- Monitor tracks login successes for either SSO and password logins.

title: Use Monitor Alert History
text:
Learn how to use the Docusign Monitor Alert History to review your
organization’s alert
history.
Additionally, you can take investigative action based on the alert
activity..

Use Alert History to quickly review Monitor alerts over a chosen time frame. You can see
who was involved, and decide what to do next without leaving the page. With the Alert
History page, you can:

- **See investigation details at a glance**

  Shows key information, such as alert name, user, account, severity, and tailored
  recommendations. You no longer must open each alert to get basic context.
- **Take action directly from the alert list**

  The overflow (three-dot) menu on each alert row has options that let you take action
  without leaving Monitor.
- **Filter and export the data that you need**

  You can filter alerts by date range, alert type, severity, and account. You can
  download a CSV snapshot of the results for audits, reporting, or offline
  analysis.
- **Stay focused on the riskiest activity**

  Severity and recommendations help you quickly distinguish routine activity from
  potential threats. You can prioritize investigation time where it matters
  most.
- **Improve future alert quality**

  The **Report false positive** option lets you flag alerts that represent
  acceptable behavior to helps us improve Monitor’s alert accuracy over time.

Here's how to access Alert History.

1. From your Docusign home page, select Admin > Organization > Monitor from the side menu.
2. Select Alert History to display alerts.

   Tip: You can refine the alert
   display details by:
   - Selecting the date range
   - Specifying one or more alerts

   ![A screenshot of a sample Monitor Alert History page.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/gso1581445176035/page/images/mon-new-alert-hx.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTc0MjQsInNoZWFmIjoiZ3NvMTU4MTQ0NTE3NjAzNSJ9.4UMSPgoC74T4xG86aENFuwReKZCIQVLDY84GDzQ9Jag&_LANG=enus)
3. Optionally select additional actions
   in
   the overflow (three-dot) menu
   for
   each alert row in the Alert History display. You can quickly investigate and manage
   alerts without
   leaving
   the
   Monitor.

   ![A screenshot of the Alert History overflow menu options.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/gso1581445176035/page/images/mon-al-hx-menu.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTc0MjQsInNoZWFmIjoiZ3NvMTU4MTQ0NTE3NjAzNSJ9.4UMSPgoC74T4xG86aENFuwReKZCIQVLDY84GDzQ9Jag&_LANG=enus)

heading_path: Set Up Notifications in Docusign Monitor Settings
text:
View, enable, or disable Docusign Monitor security alerts for your
deployment .

Use this feature to view, turn on, and turn off

Use Monitor Settings to select alerts that provide notifications about

title: Protect Your Docusign Deployment with Docusign Monitor
heading_path: Protect Your Docusign Deployment with Docusign Monitor
text:
Learn about how Docusign Monitor tracks account events and sends alerts when specific
account activity thresholds occur. Additionally, the Docusign Monitor API delivers activity
information directly to your existing SIEM.

title: Protect Your Docusign Deployment with Docusign Monitor
heading_path: Protect Your Docusign Deployment with Docusign Monitor
text:
As a Docusign organization administrator, you want to protect your
organization from outside and inside threats.

Monitor enables organization admins to receive real-time alerts about suspicious user
activities within Docusign eSignature, CLM, Workflow Builder , and
Agreement Manager.

Monitor can provide timely security notifications delivered directly to your existing
security and event management (SIEM) system or visualization tool to take quick actions
to mitigate threats such as:

- External events
  - Anomalous activity indicating a security breach
  - Access from unknown IP addresses
  - Suspicious patterns by a third party
  - Logins to an account from multiple locations
- Internal events
  - Fraudulent behavior in legitimate accounts, such as a user deleting or
    downloading an unusually high volume of envelopes
  - Disabled recipient authentication, indicating an insider-assisted attack or unauthorized
    access
  - Risk-related errors made by well-meaning users, such as too many login attempts.

With Monitor, you can actively track these types of account incidents. It provides alerts
from potential malicious activities for your Docusign agreements and
offers automatic recommendations to counteract these threats.

With Monitor, you get the Docusign Monitor API that lets you:

- Investigate third-party communication to understand whether suspicious activity occurs.
- Deliver activity information directly to your SIEM. You can customize dashboards and alerts in
  SIEM software to meet your specific industry, security, and regulatory
  requirements.

Docusign Monitor saves alert, events, and alert history data for ten years.

This video describes how Docusign Monitor helps you protect your organization.

[Introducing Docusign Monitor](https://dsucustomers.docusign.com/get-started-with-docusign-monitor/1782107)

title: Adjust Docusign Monitor Alert Sensitivity and Thresholds
heading_path: Adjust Docusign Monitor Alert Sensitivity and Thresholds
text:
Configure Docusign Monitor deployment alert sensitivity or event thresholds to
customize when and how Monitor generates your organization alerts.

You can adjust Monitor alert sensitivity or event thresholds. You can generate alerts based on
your:

- Organization size
- Activity

Here are some examples of adjusting alert sensitivity options.

| Item | Description | Example |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Maximum allowable events | Specify a new threshold for generating an alert. Set the maximum number of documents that is considered to be anomalous during one hour. | Unusual envelope download volume alert |
| Distance Threshold | Specify the minimum separation distance in KM for inclusion. Set the minimum number ( KM) that generates the alert over the last hour. For example, when a user logs in from a location that is more than 100 km from any other location where they logged on. | Simultaneous login alert |

See [eSignature Alerts](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gso1581445176035&topicId=crj1659119600990.html&_LANG=enus "Review the details for Docusign Monitor eSignature events.") and [CLM Alerts](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gso1581445176035&topicId=refccb593b2-ea43-40d9-93bd-5655e2162e5c.html&_LANG=enus "Review the details for the Docusign Monitor CLM events.") for detailed
information about alert thresholds you can change.

title: Adjust Docusign Monitor Alert Sensitivity and Thresholds
heading_path: Adjust Docusign Monitor Alert Sensitivity and Thresholds
text:
The default severity level for all alerts is set to Auto. You can
change an alert's severity level to High,
Moderate, or Low depending on your
business needs as described in the [Change an Alert's Default Risk Status](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gso1581445176035&topicId=lop1659713225796.html&_LANG=enus "Learn about changing Docusign Monitor alerts' default risk status to refine the number and frequency of the alerts you receive.") article.

title: Configure Monitor Data Search Macros
text:
Use search macros to easily search and find Monitor data.

Set up a search macro for each Monitor data type.
All the scheduled searches and dashboards within the Docusign App and Docusign Add-on use these macros. Therefore, it is important to define the
proper index and source type in each macro.

1. From the App, select Settings  > Search > Advanced > Macros.

   ![A screenshot of sample Splunk search macros for Monitor.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/gso1581445176035/page/images/monitor_splunk_config_macros.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTc0OTUsInNoZWFmIjoiZ3NvMTU4MTQ0NTE3NjAzNSJ9.Vut0WZQJ-d7vCTTzNB-qjyd6w5PplqdRGlctRscDzNo&_LANG=enus)

   Note: Replace
   index=docusign with the index name for your data.
   This name is customer specific.
2. Enter these inputs for each macro.

   | Macro | Sourcetype | Example |
   | --- | --- | --- |
   | docusign\_account\_data | docusign:accounts | index=docusign sourcetype=docusign:accounts |
   | docusign\_monitor\_data | docusign:monitor | index=docusign sourcetype=docusign:monitor |
   | docusign\_user\_data | docusign:users | index=docusign sourcetype=docusign:users |

title: Error Message: Could Not Deserialize Key Data Error
heading_path: Error Message: Could Not Deserialize Key Data Error
text:
Troubleshoot an error that occurred when the system could not deserialize key data for
Docusign Monitor Splunk integration data inputs.

This error occurs when the system cannot deserialize key data for Docusign Monitor
Splunk data inputs.

You receive this error message:

```
Could not deserialize key data.
```

You entered an incomplete RSA key when configuring Monitor data inputs.

title: Error Message: Could Not Deserialize Key Data Error
heading_path: Error Message: Could Not Deserialize Key Data Error
text:
1. Make sure you copy or enter the entire RSA key, including the
   ---Begin--- and
   –-End--- portions.
2. See these resources:

   - [Apps and keys](https://support.docusign.com/guides/ndse-admin-guide-api-and-keys)
   - [How to get an access token with JWT Grant
     authentication](https://developers.docusign.com/platform/auth/jwt/jwt-get-token/)

title: Access Monitor from Docusign Admin
text:
Learn how to access Docusign from Docusign
Admin.

As a Docusign organization administrator, use Docusign Admin to enable and manage Monitor, as described in the [Introduction to Docusign
Admin](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=rrf1583359212854&topicId=oum1583359129731.html&_LANG=enus) article.
Make sure that your organization has a license for Monitor. Contact your account team to
confirm.

Only users with Docusign Administrator or Security Reports Administrator permissions can access and manage Monitor. For more information, see the [Docusign Administrator Permissions](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=rrf1583359212854&topicId=dpo1583359153647.html&_LANG=enus) article.

To enable the paid version of Docusign Monitor from Docusign Admin:

1. From your Docusign home page, select Admin > Organization > Monitor from the side menu.
2. Select Actions > ENABLE.

   The  Monitor Enable Organization Feature dialog box displays. ![A screenshot of the Enable Docusign Monitor feature dialog box.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/gso1581445176035/page/images/monnewaccessmonitor.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTczODIsInNoZWFmIjoiZ3NvMTU4MTQ0NTE3NjAzNSJ9.3xovWkPDvULTJQooKsspFtWYbLsWNI5PlVrLhXYVlns&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Enable Docusign Monitor feature dialog box.")

   Contact your account team for assistance if:

   - You do not see the option to enable or disable Docusign Monitor.
   - The message `Not Available` displays.
3. Select ENABLE.

   A confirmation message displays.

   ![A screenshot of the Docusign Monitor successfully enabled confirmation message.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/gso1581445176035/page/images/monitorenableconfirm.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTczODIsInNoZWFmIjoiZ3NvMTU4MTQ0NTE3NjAzNSJ9.3xovWkPDvULTJQooKsspFtWYbLsWNI5PlVrLhXYVlns&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Docusign Monitor successfully enabled confirmation message.")

You have activated Monitor. Access the feature in the Docusign Admin dashboard's Monitor section to view and manage
organization alerts.

Note:

If you're using Docusign Monitor Free Edition, it's automatically enabled and you do not need a license. Access the feature in the Docusign Admin dashboard's Monitor section.

title: Monitor API Authentication
text:
Learn about how the Monitor API authenticates calls through OAuth 2.0 authentication and
a JWT grant.

The Monitor API authenticates all calls using a variant of the JSON Web Token
(JWT) grant. Customer service integrations can exchange a JWT containing organization
and scope data for an access token that authenticates calls to the API.

See the [Authentication](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/monitor-api/monitor101/auth/) article for information about
authenticating this API.

title: Monitor Workflow Builder Alerts
text:

title: Monitor CLM Alerts
text:
Review descriptions and recommended actions for Docusign Monitor CLM alerts.

CLM administrators can use Monitor to track document activity, user activity, and settings changes. See the [Track CLM Document Activities from Docusign Monitor](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=alb1642965087302&topicId=dcd1687284122199.html&_LANG=enus) article for detailed information.

| Alert | Generated when: | Recommendations |
| --- | --- | --- |
| CLM documents deleted | A user deleted more than n  documents in 1 hour. The `n` is a configurable threshold. The default is 20. | Investigate user activity and contact the user if this behavior is unusual. |
| CLM documents downloaded | A user downloaded more than n  documents in 1 hour. The `n` is a configurable threshold. The default is 20. | Investigate user activity and contact the user if this behavior is unusual. |
| CLM security settings modified | CLM folder security or Idle session timeout settings were changed. | Verify that settings changes were intentional. If this was not authorized, close the user's membership, as described in the [Manage Docusign CLM Support user permissions](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pxt1643324456371&topicId=qfh1617310293710.html&_LANG=enus) article. |

title: Docusign Monitor eSignature Alerts
text:
Review descriptions and recommended actions for Docusign Monitor eSignature, Admin settings, and login and security alerts.

Here are Docusign Monitor eSignature, Admin settings, Login and security alert descriptions and recommended actions.

Note: If the alert actions were unauthorized, you can:

- [Remove Users from Accounts](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=uwb1583277426266.html&_LANG=enus).
- Change or revoke the user's administrative privileges as described in the [Delegated Permissions](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=gih1583277347533.html&_LANG=enus) article.

title: Docusign Monitor eSignature Alerts
text:
Administrators can use Monitor to track these eSignature activity alerts.

| Alert | Generated when: | Recommendations |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Autoplace scope changed | User changed [Autoplace](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gbo1643332197980&topicId=ytr1578456405118.html&_LANG=enus) settings. | Verify that settings changes were intentional. |
| Bulk envelope sent | User successfully initiates an envelope bulk send at least `n` times. The `n` is a configurable threshold. The default is one. | Investigate user activity and contact the user if this behavior is unusual. |
| Possible signer impersonation | Matching IP, device type, application, and browser fingerprint suggest the sender signed the envelope with the signer's account. | Investigate whether the sender had a valid reason to use the same device as the signer. |
| Recipient authentication disabled | Updated account security policy. Note: This alert is available for the Monitor Free Edition. | Verify that settings changes were intentional. |
| Unusual document download volume | User downloads more than `n` envelopes in an hour.  The `n` is a configurable threshold. The default is 15. | Investigate user activity and contact the user if this behavior is unusual. |
| Unusual envelope deletion volume | User deletes more than `n` envelopes in an hour.  The `n` is a configurable threshold. The default is 20.  Note: This alert is available for the Monitor Free Edition. | Investigate user activity and contact the user if this behavior is unusual. Note: This alert's alert threshold currently includes draft envelopes. Starting in July, it will only consider sent and completed envelopes. |

title: Docusign Monitor eSignature Alerts
text:
Administrators can use Monitor to track these Monitor Administration settings activities.

| Alert | Generated when: | Recommendations |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Bulk account settings export | The user initiated a bulk accounts settings export. | Ensure that this settings data was only shared with the right users. |
| Bulk users export | User initiated a bulk export of user data, including name, email, and permission profile. | Ensure that this sensitive data was only shared to the right users. |
| Delegated administrators assigned | Assigned `n` or more Account delegated administrators in one hour. The `n` is a configurable threshold. The default is five. | Verify that permission changes were intentional. |
| Document retention policy changed | Changed the account's document retention. | Verify that settings changes were intentional. |
| Legal disclosure setting changed | Changed the account's legal disclosure setting | Verify that settings changes were intentional. |
| Watermark configuration changed | Changed the account's watermark configuration | Verify that settings changes were intentional. Review your account's watermark settings as described in the [Set Watermarks](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=zoa1644444718595.html&_LANG=enus) article. |

title: Docusign Monitor eSignature Alerts
text:
Administrators can use Monitor to track these login and security activities.

| Alert | Generated when: | Recommendations |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Multiple login failures | A user had `n` or more login failures in the past hour. The `n` is a configurable threshold. The default is six. Multiple failed logins within **1 minute** count as one failure.  Note: [Claim at least one domain](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=rrf1583359212854&topicId=gso1583359141256.html&_LANG=enus). | Reset the user's password and enforce SSO to improve security. |
| Non-domain member activity | Actions involving a non-domain user activity were detected This alert is only generates if the action was taken by the non-domain user, regardless of the event recipient.  Note: [Claim at least one domain](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=rrf1583359212854&topicId=gso1583359141256.html&_LANG=enus). | Claim this domain to centralize control and manage the user's access. |
| Password changed from new location | The user changed their password from a location more than `m`  km from any location that they have logged in from in the previous 21 days. The `n` is a configurable threshold.  The default is 200 km.  Note: This alert is available for the Monitor Free Edition. | Reset the user's password if this was unauthorized and enforce SSO to improve security. |
| Password rules changed | The account's [password rules](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=yfn1583277328637.html&_LANG=enus) were modified within one hour. | Verify that settings changes were intentional and enforce SSO to improve security. |
| Simultaneous login | The user logged on from a location more than `n` km from any location to which they’ve logged on over the last hour. The `n` is a configurable threshold. The default is 200 km.  Note: [Claim at least one domain](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=rrf1583359212854&topicId=gso1583359141256.html&_LANG=enus). | Check if this IP address and location is known and contact the user if this behavior is unusual. This alert now incorporates browser fingerprint data. It only generates when sessions occur from different devices. |
| SSO bypass enabled for user | A user was permitted to use Username/Password logon instead of SSO. Note: [Claim at least one domain](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=rrf1583359212854&topicId=gso1583359141256.html&_LANG=enus). | Verify that settings changes were intentional and enforce SSO to improve security. |
| SSO login policy changed | Changed SSO login policy of a claimed domain. Note: [Claim at least one domain](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=rrf1583359212854&topicId=gso1583359141256.html&_LANG=enus). | Verify that settings changes were intentional and enforce SSO to improve security. |
| New proxy usage | A user used a new proxy to mask their IP address and location. | Check whether this IP address and location are known and contact the user if this behavior is unusual. |
| Unauthorized IP address activity | Activity was detected from locations not included in the  [IP Safe List](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gso1581445176035&topicId=mfq1645630427780.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to create a list of trusted IP addresses with the Docusign Monitor IP Safe List feature."). | Investigate user activity and update the IP Safe List as needed. |

title: Install Docusign App and Docusign Add-on
heading_path: Install Docusign App and Docusign Add-on
text:
Learn how to install the Splunk Docusign App and Docusign Add-on.

title: Integrate Docusign Monitor Security Notifications with Splunk
heading_path: Integrate Docusign Monitor Security Notifications with Splunk
Splunk Enterprise 9.3+compatibility and the Docusign Monitor app support
text:
Due to an important change in Splunk Enterprise 9.3 and later, Python 3.7 is no longer supported. Python 3.9 is now required.

Monitor Splunk apps use third-party libraries that depend on specific Python versions, such as JWT and cryptography. Therefore, most customers are updating to the newest Splunk versions. Therefore, Docusign now only supports the latest Splunk releases.

If you are still using Splunk Enterprise versions 8.x, 9.1, and 9.2, continue using Monitor App versions 1.0.10 or earlier. These versions are compatible with Python 3.7.

heading_path: Integrate Docusign Monitor Security Notifications with Splunk
text:
The Docusign App and Docusign Add-on integrate with Docusign Monitor as follows:

- Configure and run scheduled searches to produce lookup tables.
- Use the Add-on lookup tables in automatic lookups and macros to enrich Docusign Monitor data. The lookup tables add user account information and alert and event details.

Docusign Monitor apps support the Splunk Enterprise SDK for Python Version 2.0.2.

title: Integrate Docusign Monitor Security Notifications with Splunk
heading_path: Integrate Docusign Monitor Security Notifications with Splunk
text:
Before your Splunk application can make calls to the Monitor API, it must authenticate and obtain an access token. Submit this access token that proves your application’s identity and authorization, with each request.

The Monitor API authenticates all calls using a variant of the JSON Web Token (JWT) grant. This authentication is described in the [How to get an access token with JWT Grant](https://developers.docusign.com/platform/auth/jwt/jwt-get-token/) article. Customer integrations can exchange a JWT containing organization and scope data for an access token that authenticates calls to the API.

The process of authenticating to use the Monitor API is identical to the process for calling the eSignature REST API. This process uses the JWT Grant, with these exceptions:

- The impersonated user must be a Docusign organization administrator or have the Security Reports Administrator permissions profile. For information about permission profiles, see the [Permission Profiles](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=pof1583277362435.html&_LANG=enus).
- The scopes that you request in the body of the JWT must include `signature` and `impersonation`. Additionally, you must include  `organization_read`  for Splunk.

title: Configure and Enable Alerts
text:
As a Splunk App administrator, you can configure custom alerts.

You can configure custom alerts using data from
Splunk as well as using the Docusign Monitor-generated alerts,

These
Docusign Monitor-generated alerts display in the Splunk Alert
Overview and Investigations dashboard.

- Autoplace scope changed
- Bulk accounts export
- Bulk users export
- CLM folder security modified
- Delegated administrator assigned
- Distance Threshold
- Document retention policy changed
- Envelope bulk send initiated
- Impossible travel
- Legal disclosure settings changed
- Multiple login failures
- Non-domain member activity
- Password changed from new location
- Password rules changed
- Recipient authentication disabled
- Same signer and sender
- SSO bypass enabled for user
- SSO login policy changed
- Unauthorized IP address activity
- Unusual CLM document deletion volume
- Unusual CLM document download volume
- Unusual envelope creation volume
- Unusual envelope deletion volume
- Unusual envelope download volume
- Unusual envelope send volume
- Unusual envelope sign volume
- Watermark configuration changed

The following example shows Docusign Monitor alerts.

![A screenshot of Docusign Monitor generated alerts.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/gso1581445176035/page/images/monitorsplunkdsalerts.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTc1MjQsInNoZWFmIjoiZ3NvMTU4MTQ0NTE3NjAzNSJ9.Rph7wnkhztdJYY7T5KNqIYLV3nYsoqRc3BMqvprj_6c&_LANG=enus)

Additionally, you can create your own alerts using Splunk data. For example:

- Docusign Alert - Activity Detected from Closed Account
- Docusign Alert - Block List Events

To configure alerts:

1. Enable alerts that you want to configure.
2. Configure the alert schedule.
3. Revise the time range of the alert depending on the schedule. That is, the time
   range for an alert should be the last 60 minutes if it is scheduled
   hourly.
4. Configure additional responses, such as email alerting, or a Slack
   webhook.

   This example shows the  Edit Alert dialog box.

   ![Edit an alert dialog box.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/gso1581445176035/page/images/monitorsplunkeditalert.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTc1MjQsInNoZWFmIjoiZ3NvMTU4MTQ0NTE3NjAzNSJ9.Rph7wnkhztdJYY7T5KNqIYLV3nYsoqRc3BMqvprj_6c&_LANG=enus)
5. Validate that these automatic lookups are enabled.

   - docusign:monitor : LOOKUP-docusign\_alert\_lookup
   - docusign:monitor : LOOKUP-docusign\_cim\_lookup
   - docusign:monitor : LOOKUP-docusign\_location\_lookup
   - docusign:monitor : LOOKUP-docusign\_ip\_lookup
   - docusign:monitor : LOOKUP-docusign\_user\_autolookup

   Note: There is no CIM compliance
   without automatic lookups, events correlation with user ids, or alerts correlation
   to the events that generated them.

   Here are the automatic lookups included
   in the Docusign Add-on. The alert, time, location, and user
   automatic lookups always produce new fields. The IP lookup requires user
   inputs.

   | Automatic lookup | Fields input | Field output |
   | --- | --- | --- |
   | docusign:monitor : LOOKUP-docusign\_account\_lookup | accountId | account\_name site\_id |
   | docusign:monitor : LOOKUP-docusign\_alert\_lookup | ds\_action object | alert description  severity  severity\_id |
   | docusign:monitor : LOOKUP-docusign\_cim\_lookup | ds\_action object | action data\_model  object\_category  result |
   | docusign:monitor : LOOKUP-docusign\_location\_lookup | ipAddressLocation.location | location\_blocked |
   | docusign:monitor : LOOKUP-docusign\_ip\_lookup | ipAddress | ip\_blocked |
   | docusign:monitor : LOOKUP-docusign\_user\_lookup | userId | AddedDate company  email  firstName  isAdmin  jobTitle  lastName  permissionProfileName |

  page

title: Monitor Features
text:
Learn about how Docusign Monitor provides security and which
eSignature and CLM plans support Monitor.

Using advanced analytics, Docusign provides around-the-clock cybersecurity
monitoring and notifications. Monitor can evaluate what events may be suspicious,
enabling you to quickly act upon them.

With Docusign, you can set up specific alerts for events such as:

- Compromised Credentials: Monitor detects when hackers try to log on unsuccessfully and access
  leaked credentials from other services. Dark web or paste sites commonly sell leaked
  and compromised credentials.
- Sign-ins from Anonymous or Cloud IPs: To hide their tracks, cyber criminals often
  initiate attacks by starting from an anonymous proxy or VPN. Similarly, they use
  cloud vendors (such as AWS or Azure) to initiate attacks.
- Simultaneous or suspicious logons: Attackers try to initiate sessions from geographically
  distant locations in a brief time period (such as from Seattle and Vietnam).
- Potential data extraction:
  - Users added, deactivated, assigned admin privileges, alerting administrators to changes
    made to users or their permissions and privileges
  - Atypical download or deletion of large numbers of envelopes
  - Atypical sending of large numbers (Bulk sending) of envelopes
  - Atypical downloading of large numbers (Bulk exporting) of user information

See the [Monitor alerts](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gso1581445176035&topicId=refd45c3b91-27da-49ee-aba6-f946c7e203da.html&_LANG=enus "Review descriptions and recommended actions for Docusign Monitor eSignature, Admin settings, and login and security alerts.") article for detailed information.

title: Monitor Features
text:
Docusign Monitor is available as a separate add-on for all Docusign eSignature plans
and Docusign CLM and Docusign CLM+ plans.
